STATISTICAL ARBITRAGE ON OIL ASSETS¶

Market-Neutral Portfolio Optimization with Gurobi¶

Structure:

  • Section 1 — Environment and data preparation
  • Section 2 — Statistical analysis and signal creation
  • Section 3 — Gurobi optimization model
  • Section 4 — Simulation and backtesting
  • Section 5 — Performance evaluation and visualization

Section 1 — Environment and data preparation¶

In [413]:
# Imports Section

import os
import io
import warnings
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
from scipy import stats
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller
from itertools import combinations
import yfinance as yf
from statsmodels.tsa.vector_ar.vecm import coint_johansen

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# Gurobi
try:
    import gurobipy as gp
    from gurobipy import GRB
    GUROBI_AVAILABLE = True
    print("Gurobi loaded successfully.")
except ImportError:
    GUROBI_AVAILABLE = False
    print("Gurobi not available — SciPy fallback will be used.")

Gurobi loaded successfully.

Global parameters¶

In [414]:
# Entry / exit thresholds (Z-score)
CROSSING_MAX  = 1.75     # Open position if |Z| > CROSSING_MAX
CROSSING_MEAN = 0     # Close position if |Z| < CROSSING_MEAN

# Financial parameters
ORIG_AMOUNT    = 1_000_000    # Initial capital (USD)
TX_COST        = 0.001        # Transaction cost (10bps = 0.10%) ← FIXED
RISK_FREE_RATE = 0.035        # Annual risk-free rate
LAMBDA         = 0.1          # Risk aversion (variance penalty)
STOP_LOSS_MULT = 1.25          # Stop-loss multiplier
MAX_HALF_LIFE  = 60           # Max half-life accepted (in days) ← relaxed

# Rolling window for Z-scores
ROLLING_WINDOW = 30           # days
BETA_WINDOW    = 120          # Rolling window for beta estimation

# Basket size for multivariate optimization
BASKET_SIZE    = 5            # Select 4-5 stocks for the basket

print(f"""
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Game 1 — Multivariate Statistical Arbitrage
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Initial capital     : {ORIG_AMOUNT:>12,.0f} $
  Opening threshold   :{CROSSING_MAX:>8.1f} σ
  Closing threshold   :{CROSSING_MEAN:>8.1f} σ
  Transaction cost    : {TX_COST*100:>8.2f} %  (10bps)
  Risk aversion λ     : {LAMBDA:>8.2f}
  Beta window         :{BETA_WINDOW:>8d} days
  Basket size         :{BASKET_SIZE:>8d} stocks
""")

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Game 1 — Multivariate Statistical Arbitrage
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Initial capital     :    1,000,000 $
  Opening threshold   :     1.8 σ
  Closing threshold   :     0.0 σ
  Transaction cost    :     0.10 %  (10bps)
  Risk aversion λ     :     0.10
  Beta window         :     120 days
  Basket size         :       5 stocks

Import real data via yfinance¶

In [415]:
def generate_yfinance_csv(filename="oil_market_data.csv"):

    # anchor
    anchor = ["BZ=F"]             # Brent Crude Oil Futures

    # sector proxies (ETF)
    etfs = ["XLE", "XOP", "OIH","IEZ", "AMLP"]
    # stocks
    stocks = [
        "BP", "SHEL", "TTE", "ENI", "EQNR",
        "REP", "WDS", "TLW", "PBR",
        "SU", "CNQ", "IMB.L", "EC", "GALP.LS",
        "YPF", "E", "CTRA", "DVN", "OXY",
        "SLB"
    ]

    all_tickers = anchor + etfs + stocks
    print(f"Downloading {len(all_tickers)} tickers from Yahoo Finance...")
    print(f"  Anchor : {anchor}")
    print(f"  ETFs   : {etfs}")
    print(f"  Stocks : {stocks[:10]}... ({len(stocks)} total)")

    df = yf.download(all_tickers, period="5y", progress=False)

    # Handle MultiIndex columns (new yfinance behavior)
    if isinstance(df.columns, pd.MultiIndex):
        df = df.stack(level=1, future_stack=True).reset_index()
        if 'Ticker' in df.columns:
            df.rename(columns={'Ticker': 'Name'}, inplace=True)
    else:
        df.reset_index(inplace=True)

    # Map each ticker to a numeric ID
    ticker_to_num = {t: i + 1 for i, t in enumerate(all_tickers)}
    df['Num'] = df['Name'].map(ticker_to_num)

    expected_columns = ['Date', 'Open', 'High', 'Low', 'Close', 'Volume', 'Num', 'Name']
    cols_to_keep = [c for c in expected_columns if c in df.columns]
    df = df[cols_to_keep]
    df.dropna(inplace=True)
    df.sort_values(by=['Date', 'Name'], inplace=True)

    df.to_csv(filename, index=False)
    n_assets = df['Name'].nunique()
    print(f"\nData saved to '{filename}'.")
    print(f"Total rows: {len(df):,} — {n_assets} assets detected.")
    print(f"Total: 1 anchor + 3 ETFs + {n_assets - 4} stocks = {n_assets} total")

    return df

raw_df = generate_yfinance_csv()

Downloading 26 tickers from Yahoo Finance...

Loading CSV file¶

I'm on collab

In [416]:
def load_data_from_upload():
    """Expected format: Date | Open | High | Low | Close | Volume | Num | Name"""
    try:
        from google.colab import files
        print("Select your CSV file:")
        uploaded = files.upload()
        if not uploaded:
            raise ValueError("No file uploaded.")
        filename = list(uploaded.keys())[0]
        content = uploaded[filename]
        if filename.endswith(".xlsx"):
            df = pd.read_excel(io.BytesIO(content))
        else:
            df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(content))
        print(f"File '{filename}' loaded — {len(df):,} rows, "
              f"{df['Name'].nunique()} assets detected.")
        return df
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Colab upload unavailable ({e}).")
        return None

Pivot and temporal synchronization¶

In [417]:
def prepare_price_matrix(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Pivots long => wide and filters to common dates across all assets."""

    df["Date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["Date"])
    id_col = "Name" if "Name" in df.columns else "Num"

    pivot = df.pivot_table(index="Date", columns=id_col, values="Close", aggfunc="last")
    pivot.sort_index(inplace=True)

    before = len(pivot)
    pivot.dropna(how="any", inplace=True)
    after = len(pivot)

    print(f"Temporal synchronization: {before} → {after} dates "
          f"({before - after} removed, {pivot.shape[1]} assets retained).")
    return pivot

Exec Pipeline of section 1¶

if raw_df is None:

prices = prepare_price_matrix(raw_df)

print(f"\nOverview of the price matrix:")
print(prices.tail(3).to_string())
print(f"\nPeriod: {prices.index[0].date()} → {prices.index[-1].date()}")

Identify the anchor and stock universe¶

SECTION 2 — STATISTICAL ANALYSIS AND SIGNAL CREATION¶

Logarithmic returns¶

In [420]:
log_returns = np.log(prices / prices.shift(1)).dropna()
asset_names = list(prices.columns)

print(f"Log returns calculated: {log_returns.shape[0]} days x "
      f"{log_returns.shape[1]} assets.")

Log returns calculated: 1225 days x 23 assets.

Correlation matrix¶

In [421]:
corr_matrix = log_returns.corr()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 10))
im = ax.imshow(corr_matrix.values, cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=-1, vmax=1, aspect="auto")
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, fraction=0.046, pad=0.04)

ax.set_xticks(range(len(all_assets)))
ax.set_yticks(range(len(all_assets)))
ax.set_xticklabels(all_assets, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=7)
ax.set_yticklabels(all_assets, fontsize=7)
ax.set_title("Correlation matrix — Oil assets (log returns)", fontsize=13, pad=15)

for i in range(len(all_assets)):
    for j in range(len(all_assets)):
        val = corr_matrix.values[i, j]
        ax.text(j, i, f"{val:.2f}", ha="center", va="center",
                fontsize=5.5, color="black" if abs(val) < 0.8 else "white")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Sector Beta Analysis: ETFs as Sector Proxies¶

The ETFs (XLE, XOP, OIH, IEZ, AMLP) serve as sector proxies to measure how much each stock in our basket is exposed to the broader energy sector, beyond just the Brent Crude anchor (BZ=F).

This helps validate that our Gurobi optimization removes not only commodity-level exposure (β vs BZ=F = 0) but also shows awareness of sector-level correlations.

In [422]:
# Compute correlation of basket stocks vs ETFs
basket_and_anchor = [ANCHOR_TICKER] + BASKET_STOCKS
sector_corr = log_returns[basket_and_anchor + ETF_TICKERS].corr()

# Visualize: Basket stocks vs ETFs correlation heatmap
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))

# Extract sub-matrix: rows = basket + anchor, cols = ETFs
sub_corr = sector_corr.loc[basket_and_anchor, ETF_TICKERS]

im = ax.imshow(sub_corr.values, cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=-1, vmax=1, aspect="auto")
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, fraction=0.046, pad=0.04)

ax.set_xticks(range(len(ETF_TICKERS)))
ax.set_yticks(range(len(basket_and_anchor)))
ax.set_xticklabels(ETF_TICKERS, fontsize=10)
ax.set_yticklabels(basket_and_anchor, fontsize=10)

for i in range(len(basket_and_anchor)):
    for j in range(len(ETF_TICKERS)):
        val = sub_corr.values[i, j]
        ax.text(j, i, f"{val:.2f}", ha="center", va="center",
                fontsize=11, color="black" if abs(val) < 0.8 else "white")

ax.set_title("Basket stocks vs Sector Proxy ETFs — Correlation", fontsize=13, pad=15)
ax.set_xlabel("Sector Proxies (ETFs)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel("Basket + Anchor", fontsize=11)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"  exposure to the energy sector (high correlation with XLE/XOP),")

  CNQ          +0.307      0.818      0.816
  E            +0.230      0.702      0.664

  The ETFs confirm that my basket stocks have significant
  exposure to the energy sector (high correlation with XLE/XOP),
============================================================

Beta estimation (each stock vs the Anchor)¶

In [423]:
def compute_betas(log_returns_df: pd.DataFrame, anchor: str,
                  stock_list: list, window: int = BETA_WINDOW) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Computes rolling beta of each stock relative to the anchor
    β_i = Cov(r_i, r_anchor) / Var(r_anchor)

    Returns a df: index=Date, columns=stock tickers, values=β
    """
    anchor_ret = log_returns_df[anchor]
    betas = pd.DataFrame(index=log_returns_df.index)

    for stock in stock_list:
        stock_ret = log_returns_df[stock]
        roll_cov = stock_ret.rolling(window).cov(anchor_ret)
        roll_var = anchor_ret.rolling(window).var()
        betas[stock] = roll_cov / roll_var

    betas.dropna(inplace=True)
    return betas

# All tradeable assets = ETFs + stocks
tradeable = ETF_TICKERS + stock_tickers
betas_df = compute_betas(log_returns, ANCHOR_TICKER, tradeable, window=BETA_WINDOW)

print(f"Betas computed for {len(tradeable)} assets over {len(betas_df)} days.")
print(f"\nLatest betas (last row):")
latest_betas = betas_df.iloc[-1].sort_values(ascending=False)
for ticker, beta in latest_betas.items():
    print(f"  {ticker:>6s} : β = {beta:+.3f}")

       E : β = +0.230

     SLB : β = -0.016
   IMB.L : β = -0.042

Multivariate Cointegration¶

here the selection of the best basket

In [424]:
def compute_half_life(spread: pd.Series) -> float:
    """Half-life of mean reversion via AR(1) regression on spread."""
    df_s = pd.DataFrame({"y": spread.diff(), "x": spread.shift(1)}).dropna()
    if df_s.empty:
        return np.inf
    slope, _, _, _, _ = stats.linregress(df_s["x"], df_s["y"])
    return -np.log(2) / slope if slope < 0 else np.inf


def find_best_basket(prices_df: pd.DataFrame, anchor: str,
                     candidates: list, basket_size: int = BASKET_SIZE,
                     max_combos: int = 1000) -> list:

    #Tests combinations of basket_size stocks from candidates.
    #For each combo, runs Johansen cointegration test on (anchor + combo).
    #Returns the best basket ranked by: (cointegration strength, half-life).

    anchor_prices = np.log(prices_df[anchor])
    results = []

    combos = list(combinations(candidates, basket_size))
    # If too many combinations, sample randomly
    if len(combos) > max_combos:
        rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
        indices = rng.choice(len(combos), max_combos, replace=False)
        combos = [combos[i] for i in indices]

    print(f"Testing {len(combos)} combinations of {basket_size} stocks "
          f"(from {len(candidates)} candidates)...")

    for combo in combos:
        try:
            # Build log-price matrix: anchor + basket stocks
            cols = [anchor] + list(combo)
            log_prices = np.log(prices_df[cols]).dropna()

            if len(log_prices) < 250:   # Need enough data
                continue

            # Johansen cointegration test
            joh = coint_johansen(log_prices, det_order=0, k_ar_diff=1)

            # trace_stat[0] = test for r=0 (no cointegration)
            # i need this to exceed the critical value
            trace_stat = joh.lr1[0]
            crit_95    = joh.cvt[0, 1]

            if trace_stat <= crit_95:
                continue   # Not cointegrated

            # Cointegrating vector (first eigenvector)
            coint_vec = joh.evec[:, 0]

            # Build the spread using the cointegrating vector
            spread = log_prices.values @ coint_vec
            spread = pd.Series(spread, index=log_prices.index)

            # Half-life
            hl = compute_half_life(spread)
            if hl <= 0 or hl > MAX_HALF_LIFE:
                continue

            # ADF test on the spread for extra validation
            adf_result = adfuller(spread.dropna(), autolag="AIC")
            adf_pval = adf_result[1]

            results.append({
                "basket":      list(combo),
                "trace_stat":  trace_stat,
                "crit_95":     crit_95,
                "coint_vec":   coint_vec,
                "half_life":   hl,
                "adf_pval":    adf_pval,
                "strength":    trace_stat / crit_95,
            })

        except Exception:
            continue

    if not results:
        print("No cointegrated basket found, change parameters.")
        return []

    # Sort by strength (higher = better cointegration) then half-life (lower = faster reversion)
    results.sort(key=lambda r: (-r["strength"], r["half_life"]))

    print(f"\n✓ {len(results)} cointegrated baskets found.")
    print(f"  Top basket: {results[0]['basket']}")
    print(f"    Trace stat    : {results[0]['trace_stat']:.2f} (crit 95%: {results[0]['crit_95']:.2f})")
    print(f"    Half-life     : {results[0]['half_life']:.1f} days")
    print(f"    ADF p-value   : {results[0]['adf_pval']:.4f}")

    return results


# Run the basket search (excludes etf with stock tickers)
basket_results = find_best_basket(
    prices, ANCHOR_TICKER,
    candidates=stock_tickers,
    basket_size=BASKET_SIZE,
    max_combos=2000 #to maximize the best baskets (1000 : bad baskets - 2000 : the best compromise - 3000 useless i got small improvements for 10sec more of calculus)
)

# Selection
best = basket_results[0]
BASKET_STOCKS = best["basket"]
COINT_VEC     = best["coint_vec"]

print(f"\n{'='*55}")
print(f"  Selected basket: {BASKET_STOCKS}")
print(f"  Cointegrating vector: {np.round(COINT_VEC, 4)}")
print(f"{'='*55}")

    Half-life     : 9.2 days
    ADF p-value   : 0.0000

=======================================================
  Selected basket: ['CNQ', 'E', 'OXY', 'WDS', 'YPF']
  Cointegrating vector: [  2.2529 -20.6856  17.4155  15.9232 -12.5435   1.3111]
=======================================================

Build the spread and Z-score for the chosen basket¶

In [425]:
def build_basket_spread(prices_df, anchor, basket_stocks, coint_vec, window=ROLLING_WINDOW):
    """
    Constructs the multivariate spread using the Johansen cointegrating vector.
    spread_t = sum( coint_vec_i · log(P_i,t))  for i in {anchor, basket_stocks}
    """
    cols = [anchor] + basket_stocks
    log_p = np.log(prices_df[cols]).dropna()

    spread = pd.Series(log_p.values @ coint_vec, index=log_p.index, name="Spread")

    # Rolling zscore
    mu  = spread.rolling(window).mean()
    sig = spread.rolling(window).std()
    zscore = ((spread - mu) / sig).dropna()
    zscore.name = "Z-score"

    return spread, zscore

spread_series, zscore_series = build_basket_spread(
    prices, ANCHOR_TICKER, BASKET_STOCKS, COINT_VEC
)

# Visualization
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 8), sharex=True)

ax1 = axes[0]
ax1.plot(spread_series.index, spread_series.values, linewidth=0.9, color="#1565C0")
ax1.set_ylabel("Spread (log space)")
ax1.set_title(f"Cointegrated Spread: {ANCHOR_TICKER} + {BASKET_STOCKS}", fontsize=12, pad=8)
ax1.grid(alpha=0.3)

ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.plot(zscore_series.index, zscore_series.values, linewidth=0.9, color="#2196F3")
ax2.axhline( CROSSING_MAX,  color="#E53935", ls="--", lw=1.2, label=f"Open ±{CROSSING_MAX}σ")
ax2.axhline(-CROSSING_MAX,  color="#E53935", ls="--", lw=1.2)
ax2.axhline( CROSSING_MEAN, color="#43A047", ls=":",  lw=1.0, label=f"Close ±{CROSSING_MEAN}σ")
ax2.axhline(-CROSSING_MEAN, color="#43A047", ls=":",  lw=1.0)
ax2.axhline(0, color="grey", lw=0.7)
ax2.fill_between(zscore_series.index, zscore_series.values, CROSSING_MAX,
                 where=(zscore_series.values > CROSSING_MAX),  alpha=0.2, color="#E53935")
ax2.fill_between(zscore_series.index, zscore_series.values, -CROSSING_MAX,
                 where=(zscore_series.values < -CROSSING_MAX), alpha=0.2, color="#4CAF50")
ax2.set_ylabel("Z-score")
ax2.set_title("Z-score of Basket Spread", fontsize=11)
ax2.legend(fontsize=8, loc="upper right")
ax2.grid(alpha=0.3)

for ax in axes:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%Y-%m"))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=3))
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Trade signals on Z-score chart¶

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))

ax.plot(zscore_series.index, zscore_series.values, lw=0.8, color="#2196F3", alpha=0.7)
ax.axhline(CROSSING_MAX, color="#E53935", ls="--", lw=1)
ax.axhline(-CROSSING_MAX, color="#E53935", ls="--", lw=1)

                where=(zscore_series.values > CROSSING_MAX), alpha=0.15, color="#E53935")

if not trade_log_df.empty:
    opens = trade_log_df[trade_log_df["action"] == "OPEN"]
    closes = trade_log_df[trade_log_df["action"].isin(["CLOSE", "STOP-LOSS"])]

    ax.scatter(pd.to_datetime(opens["date"]), opens["z_score"],
               marker="^", s=80, color="#2E7D32", zorder=5, label="Open", edgecolors="white", linewidth=0.5)
    ax.scatter(pd.to_datetime(closes["date"]), closes["z_score"],
               marker="v", s=80, color="#C62828", zorder=5, label="Close / Stop-Loss", edgecolors="white", linewidth=0.5)

ax.set_title("Z-score with trade entry/exit signals", fontsize=13, pad=10)
ax.set_ylabel("Z-score")

ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%Y-%m"))
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()

SECTION 3 — GUROBI OPTIMIZATION WITH Σβ = 0¶

Gurobi optimizer with beta-neutrality¶

In [427]:
def optimize_basket_weights(expected_returns: np.ndarray,
                            cov_matrix: np.ndarray,
                            betas: np.ndarray,
                            lam: float = LAMBDA,
                            tx_cost: float = TX_COST) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Gurobi optimization for market-neutral basket.

    Formulation:
        max   μᵀw  −  λ · wᵀΣw  −  tx_cost · Σ|wᵢ|

        s.t.  Σ(wᵢ · βᵢ) = 0          ← MARKET NEUTRALITY (Σβ = 0)
              Σwᵢ⁺ ≤ 1                  (long budget)
              Σ|wᵢ⁻| ≤ 1               (short budget)
              -1 ≤ wᵢ ≤ 1              (individual bounds)

    Parameters:
        expected_returns : mean log returns of basket stocks
        cov_matrix       : (n,n) covariance matrix of basket stocks
        betas            :  beta of each stock vs the anchor
        lam              : risk aversion parameter
        tx_cost          : transaction cost rate

    Returns:
        weights : optimal portfolio weights
    """
    n = len(expected_returns)

    if not GUROBI_AVAILABLE:
        return _fallback_optimizer_neutral(expected_returns, cov_matrix, betas, lam, tx_cost)

    try:
        env = gp.Env(empty=True)
        env.setParam("OutputFlag", 0)
        env.start()
        m = gp.Model(env=env)
        m.setParam("OutputFlag", 0)

        # Decision variables
        w_long  = m.addVars(n, lb=0.0,  ub=1.0,  name="w_long")
        w_short = m.addVars(n, lb=-1.0, ub=0.0,  name="w_short")
        w_abs   = m.addVars(n, lb=0.0,  ub=1.0,  name="w_abs")

        w = [w_long[i] + w_short[i] for i in range(n)]

        # Constraints

        # 1) MARKET NEUTRALITY: Σ(wᵢ · βᵢ) = 0
        m.addConstr(
            gp.quicksum(betas[i] * w[i] for i in range(n)) == 0,
            "beta_neutral"
        )

        # 2) Budget constraints
        m.addConstr(gp.quicksum(w_long[i]   for i in range(n)) <= 1.0, "budget_long")
        m.addConstr(gp.quicksum(-w_short[i]  for i in range(n)) <= 1.0, "budget_short")

        # 3) Absolute value linearization for tx cost
        for i in range(n):
            m.addConstr(w_abs[i] >=  w[i], f"abs_pos_{i}")
            m.addConstr(w_abs[i] >= -w[i], f"abs_neg_{i}")

        # Objective
        ret_term = gp.quicksum(expected_returns[i] * w[i] for i in range(n))
        cov_term = gp.quicksum(
            cov_matrix[i, j] * w[i] * w[j]
            for i in range(n) for j in range(n)
        )
        tx_term = tx_cost * gp.quicksum(w_abs[i] for i in range(n))

        m.setObjective(ret_term - lam * cov_term - tx_term, GRB.MAXIMIZE)
        m.optimize()

        if m.status == GRB.OPTIMAL:
            weights = np.array([w_long[i].X + w_short[i].X for i in range(n)])
            # Verify beta neutrality
            port_beta = np.dot(weights, betas)
            print(f"  Gurobi optimal — Portfolio β = {port_beta:.6f} (target: 0)")
        else:
            print(f"  Gurobi status: {m.status}. Using fallback.")
            weights = _fallback_optimizer_neutral(expected_returns, cov_matrix,
                                                  betas, lam, tx_cost)

        m.dispose()
        env.dispose()
        return weights

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  Gurobi error: {e}. Using SciPy fallback.")
        return _fallback_optimizer_neutral(expected_returns, cov_matrix,
                                           betas, lam, tx_cost)


def _fallback_optimizer_neutral(expected_returns, cov_matrix, betas, lam, tx_cost):
    """SciPy fallback with beta-neutrality constraint."""
    from scipy.optimize import minimize

    n = len(expected_returns)

    def neg_objective(w):
        ret = expected_returns @ w
        var = w @ cov_matrix @ w
        tc  = tx_cost * np.sum(np.abs(w))
        return -(ret - lam * var - tc)

    constraints = [
        {"type": "eq",  "fun": lambda w: np.dot(w, betas)},               # Σβ = 0
        {"type": "ineq","fun": lambda w: 1.0 - np.sum(np.maximum(w, 0))}, # long budget
        {"type": "ineq","fun": lambda w: 1.0 - np.sum(np.maximum(-w, 0))},# short budget
    ]
    bounds = [(-1.0, 1.0)] * n
    w0     = np.zeros(n)
    result = minimize(neg_objective, w0, method="SLSQP",
                      bounds=bounds, constraints=constraints,
                      options={"maxiter": 500, "ftol": 1e-9})
    return result.x if result.success else w0

Quick optimizer test¶

In [428]:
_n = 5
_mu   = np.random.uniform(-0.01, 0.02, _n)
_cov  = np.eye(_n) * 0.0004
_beta = np.array([0.8, 1.2, 0.5, -0.3, 0.9])  # Example betas

w_test = optimize_basket_weights(_mu, _cov, _beta, lam=LAMBDA, tx_cost=TX_COST)
print(f"\n  Test weights  : {np.round(w_test, 4)}")
print(f"  Sum of longs  : {np.sum(np.maximum(w_test, 0)):.4f}")
print(f"  Sum of shorts : {np.sum(np.minimum(w_test, 0)):.4f}")
print(f"  Portfolio β   : {np.dot(w_test, _beta):.6f}  (if 0 is good)")

  Gurobi optimal — Portfolio β = 0.000000 (target: 0)

  Test weights  : [0.2727 0.     0.     0.7273 0.    ]
  Sum of longs  : 1.0000
  Sum of shorts : 0.0000
  Portfolio β   : 0.000000  (if 0 is good)

SECTION 4 — SIMULATION AND BACKTESTING¶

Main backtesting function¶

In [429]:
def simulate_basket_strategy(prices_df: pd.DataFrame,
                             anchor: str,
                             basket_stocks: list,
                             betas_df: pd.DataFrame,
                             zscore_series: pd.Series,
                             orig_amount: float = ORIG_AMOUNT,
                             tx_cost: float = TX_COST,
                             cross_max: float = CROSSING_MAX,
                             cross_mean: float = CROSSING_MEAN,
                             lam: float = LAMBDA,
                             rebalance_freq: int = 40) -> dict: #was 10 but cost too much in transaction
    """
    Backtests the market-neutral basket strategy

    Logic:
        1. Every rebalance_freq days, re-optimize weights using Gurobi (Σβ=0)
        2. When |Z-score| > cross_max → open position (long or short the basket)
        3. When |Z-score| < cross_mean → close position
        4. Stop-loss if |Z-score| > cross_max * STOP_LOSS_MULT

    The basket is traded as a single unit with optimized weights.
    """
    log_ret = np.log(prices_df / prices_df.shift(1))

    # Align all series
    common_dates = zscore_series.dropna().index.intersection(betas_df.index)
    common_dates = common_dates.intersection(log_ret.index).sort_values()

    capital        = orig_amount
    portfolio_vals = {}
    trade_log      = []
    position       = None       # None = flat - dict = open position
    current_weights = None
    last_rebalance  = 0

    print(f"Simulating over {len(common_dates)} trading days...")
    print(f"  Rebalance every {rebalance_freq} days")

    for i, date in enumerate(common_dates):
        z = zscore_series.loc[date]
        if np.isnan(z):
            portfolio_vals[date] = capital
            continue

        #  Periodic weight rebalancing
        if current_weights is None or (i - last_rebalance) >= rebalance_freq:
            # Get current betas for basket stocks
            if date in betas_df.index:
                curr_betas = betas_df.loc[date, basket_stocks].values
            else:
                portfolio_vals[date] = capital
                continue

            # Skip if any beta is NaN
            if np.any(np.isnan(curr_betas)):
                portfolio_vals[date] = capital
                continue

            # Lookback for expected returns and covariance
            lookback = 60
            idx_pos  = log_ret.index.get_loc(date)
            if idx_pos < lookback:
                portfolio_vals[date] = capital
                continue

            ret_window = log_ret.loc[log_ret.index[idx_pos-lookback:idx_pos], basket_stocks]
            mu_est     = ret_window.mean().values
            cov_est    = ret_window.cov().values

            # Optimize with Σβ = 0
            current_weights = optimize_basket_weights(
                mu_est, cov_est, curr_betas, lam=lam, tx_cost=tx_cost
            )
            last_rebalance = i

            # Apply transaction cost for rebalancing
            if position is not None:
                tc = position["allocation"] * tx_cost
                capital -= tc

        if current_weights is None:
            portfolio_vals[date] = capital
            continue

        #  Daily PnL for open position
        if position is not None:
            alloc = position["allocation"]
            daily_rets = log_ret.loc[date, basket_stocks].values

            if position["side"] == "long_basket":
                pnl = alloc * np.dot(current_weights, daily_rets)
            else:  # short_basket
                pnl = alloc * np.dot(-current_weights, daily_rets)

            capital += pnl

            #  Closing logic
            if abs(z) >= cross_max * STOP_LOSS_MULT:
                tc = alloc * tx_cost
                capital -= tc
                trade_log.append({
                    "date": date, "action": "STOP-LOSS",
                    "side": position["side"], "z_score": round(z, 4),
                    "allocation": alloc, "tx_cost": tc, "pnl_est": round(pnl, 2),
                    "port_beta": round(float(np.dot(current_weights, curr_betas)), 4),
                })
                position = None

            elif abs(z) < cross_mean:
                tc = alloc * tx_cost
                capital -= tc
                trade_log.append({
                    "date": date, "action": "CLOSE",
                    "side": position["side"], "z_score": round(z, 4),
                    "allocation": alloc, "tx_cost": tc, "pnl_est": round(pnl, 2),
                    "port_beta": round(float(np.dot(current_weights, curr_betas)), 4),
                })
                position = None

        #  Opening logic
        elif abs(z) > cross_max and capital > 0:
            side = "long_basket" if z < -cross_max else "short_basket"
            allocation = min(capital * 0.2, capital) # max 20% per position (leverage by 2x)
            tc = allocation * tx_cost
            capital -= tc

            position = {
                "side":       side,
                "entry_z":    z,
                "entry_date": date,
                "allocation": allocation,
            }
            trade_log.append({
                "date": date, "action": "OPEN",
                "side": side, "z_score": round(z, 4),
                "allocation": allocation, "tx_cost": tc,
                "weights": np.round(current_weights, 4).tolist(),
                "port_beta": round(float(np.dot(current_weights,
                    betas_df.loc[date, basket_stocks].values if date in betas_df.index else curr_betas)), 4),
            })

        portfolio_vals[date] = capital

    # Post-processing
    portfolio_series = pd.Series(portfolio_vals).sort_index()
    portfolio_series.name = "Portfolio"

    trade_log_df = pd.DataFrame(trade_log) if trade_log else pd.DataFrame()
    n_trades  = len([t for t in trade_log if t["action"] == "OPEN"])
    final_val = portfolio_series.iloc[-1]
    total_ret = (final_val - orig_amount) / orig_amount * 100

    print(f"""
{'='*55}
  Backtesting Results — Market-Neutral Basket
{'='*55}
  Initial capital  : ${orig_amount:>12,.0f}
  Final capital    : ${final_val:>12,.0f}
  Total return     : {total_ret:>+11.2f} %
  Number of trades : {n_trades:>12,}
  Basket stocks    : {basket_stocks}
{'='*55}
""")

    return {
        "portfolio_values": portfolio_series,
        "trade_log": trade_log_df,
    }

Run the simu¶

In [430]:
sim_results = simulate_basket_strategy(
    prices_df     = prices,
    anchor        = ANCHOR_TICKER,
    basket_stocks = BASKET_STOCKS,
    betas_df      = betas_df,
    zscore_series = zscore_series,
    orig_amount   = ORIG_AMOUNT,
    tx_cost       = TX_COST,
    cross_max     = CROSSING_MAX,
    cross_mean    = CROSSING_MEAN,
    lam           = LAMBDA,
    rebalance_freq = 20,
)

portfolio_values = sim_results["portfolio_values"]
trade_log_df     = sim_results["trade_log"]

Portfolio Beta Over Time¶

if not trade_log_df.empty and "port_beta" in trade_log_df.columns:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 3.5))

    beta_log = trade_log_df[["date", "port_beta"]].copy()
    beta_log["date"] = pd.to_datetime(beta_log["date"])

    ax.scatter(beta_log["date"], beta_log["port_beta"], s=30, color="#1565C0",
               alpha=0.8, edgecolors="white", linewidth=0.5, zorder=5)
    ax.axhline(0, color="#2E7D32", lw=2, ls="-", label="Target β = 0")
    ax.fill_between(beta_log["date"], -0.01, 0.01, alpha=0.1, color="#2E7D32")

    ax.set_title("Portfolio Beta at Each Trade — Market Neutrality Verification", fontsize=13, pad=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("Portfolio β")
    ax.set_ylim(-0.05, 0.05)
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%Y-%m"))
    plt.xticks(rotation=30)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

SECTION 5 — PERFORMANCE EVALUATION AND VISUALIZATION¶

Simple Benchmark: without strategy¶

Same basket, equal weights, no beta-neutrality

In [432]:
def simulate_naive_basket(prices_df, anchor, basket_stocks, zscore_series,
                          orig_amount=ORIG_AMOUNT, tx_cost=TX_COST,
                          cross_max=CROSSING_MAX, cross_mean=CROSSING_MEAN):
    """
    Same Z-score signals, same basket, but with equal weights (1/n).
    No Gurobi optimization, no beta-neutrality.
    This is the 'simple benchmark' to show the value of optimization.
    """
    n = len(basket_stocks)
    naive_weights = np.ones(n) / n   # Equal weight: 1/5 each

    log_ret = np.log(prices_df / prices_df.shift(1))
    common_dates = zscore_series.dropna().index.intersection(log_ret.index).sort_values()

    capital = orig_amount
    portfolio_vals = {}
    position = None

    for date in common_dates:
        z = zscore_series.loc[date]
        if np.isnan(z):
            portfolio_vals[date] = capital
            continue

        # Daily PnL for open position
        if position is not None:
            daily_rets = log_ret.loc[date, basket_stocks].values
            if position["side"] == "long_basket":
                pnl = position["allocation"] * np.dot(naive_weights, daily_rets)
            else:
                pnl = position["allocation"] * np.dot(-naive_weights, daily_rets)
            capital += pnl

            # Closing
            if abs(z) >= cross_max * STOP_LOSS_MULT:
                capital -= position["allocation"] * tx_cost
                position = None
            elif abs(z) < cross_mean:
                capital -= position["allocation"] * tx_cost
                position = None

        # Opening
        elif abs(z) > cross_max and capital > 0:
            side = "long_basket" if z < -cross_max else "short_basket"
            allocation = min(capital * 0.2, capital)
            capital -= allocation * tx_cost
            position = {"side": side, "allocation": allocation}

        portfolio_vals[date] = capital

    result = pd.Series(portfolio_vals).sort_index()
    result.name = "Naive Equal-Weight Basket"
    return result

naive_values = simulate_naive_basket(
    prices, ANCHOR_TICKER, BASKET_STOCKS, zscore_series
)
naive_metrics, naive_drawdown = compute_performance_metrics(naive_values)

Performance metrics¶

In [433]:
def compute_performance_metrics(portfolio: pd.Series,
                                risk_free_rate: float = RISK_FREE_RATE,
                                orig_amount: float = ORIG_AMOUNT) -> tuple:
    """Sharpe, CAGR, Max Drawdown, Calmar, volatility."""
    daily_ret = portfolio.pct_change().dropna()
    n_days    = len(portfolio)
    n_years   = n_days / 252

    total_return      = (portfolio.iloc[-1] / orig_amount) - 1
    annualized_return = (1 + total_return) ** (1 / n_years) - 1

    daily_vol  = daily_ret.std()
    annual_vol = daily_vol * np.sqrt(252)
    daily_rf   = risk_free_rate / 252

    excess_ret = daily_ret - daily_rf
    sharpe     = (excess_ret.mean() / excess_ret.std()) * np.sqrt(252) \
                 if excess_ret.std() > 0 else 0.0

    cum_max  = portfolio.cummax()
    drawdown = (portfolio - cum_max) / cum_max
    max_dd   = drawdown.min()

    calmar  = annualized_return / abs(max_dd) if abs(max_dd) > 0 else np.inf
    dd_days = (drawdown < 0).sum()

    metrics = {
        "Total Return (%)":          round(total_return * 100, 2),
        "CAGR (%)":                  round(annualized_return * 100, 2),
        "Annualized Volatility (%)": round(annual_vol * 100, 2),
        "Sharpe Ratio":              round(sharpe, 3),
        "Max Drawdown (%)":          round(max_dd * 100, 2),
        "Calmar Ratio":              round(calmar, 3),
        "Days in Drawdown":          int(dd_days),
        "Final Capital ($)":         round(portfolio.iloc[-1], 2),
    }
    return metrics, drawdown

Benchmark (equal-weight buy & hold)¶

In [434]:
def compute_equal_weight_benchmark(prices_df, orig_amount=ORIG_AMOUNT):
    daily_returns = prices_df.pct_change().dropna().mean(axis=1)
    bh = orig_amount * (1 + daily_returns).cumprod()
    bh = bh.reindex(portfolio_values.index).ffill()
    bh.name = "Benchmark (Equal-Weight)"
    return bh

bh_values = compute_equal_weight_benchmark(prices)

strat_metrics, strat_drawdown = compute_performance_metrics(portfolio_values)
bh_metrics,    bh_drawdown    = compute_performance_metrics(bh_values)

Chart Strategy vs Naive vs B&H — Price Index + Drawdown¶

In [435]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 11), gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [2.5, 1]})

strat_idx = portfolio_values / ORIG_AMOUNT * 100
naive_idx = naive_values.reindex(portfolio_values.index).ffill() / ORIG_AMOUNT * 100
bh_idx    = bh_values / ORIG_AMOUNT * 100

ax1 = axes[0]
ax1.plot(strat_idx.index, strat_idx.values, lw=2.0, color="#1565C0",
         label="Optimized Basket (Gurobi, Σβ=0)")
ax1.plot(naive_idx.index, naive_idx.values, lw=1.5, ls="-.",
         color="#7B1FA2", alpha=0.85, label="Naive Basket (Equal-Weight, no optimization)")
ax1.plot(bh_idx.index, bh_idx.values, lw=1.5, ls="--",
         color="#E65100", alpha=0.85, label="Buy & Hold (All Assets)")
ax1.axhline(100, color="grey", lw=0.8, ls=":")
ax1.set_ylabel("Price index (base 100)")
ax1.set_title("Optimized Basket vs Simple Benchmarks", fontsize=13, pad=10)
ax1.legend(fontsize=9, loc="upper left")
ax1.grid(alpha=0.3)

bbox = dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.4", facecolor="white", edgecolor="#1565C0", alpha=0.85)
annot = (f"CAGR : {strat_metrics['CAGR (%)']:+.1f}%\n"
         f"Sharpe : {strat_metrics['Sharpe Ratio']:.2f}\n"
         f"Max DD : {strat_metrics['Max Drawdown (%)']:.1f}%\n"
         f"Calmar : {strat_metrics['Calmar Ratio']:.2f}")
ax1.annotate(annot, xy=(0.98, 0.03), xycoords="axes fraction",
             fontsize=9, verticalalignment="top", bbox=bbox)

ax2 = axes[1]
strat_dd = strat_drawdown.reindex(strat_idx.index).fillna(0) * 100
naive_dd = naive_drawdown.reindex(naive_idx.index).fillna(0) * 100
bh_dd    = bh_drawdown.reindex(bh_idx.index).fillna(0) * 100

ax2.fill_between(strat_dd.index, strat_dd.values, 0, alpha=0.5,
                 color="#1565C0", label="Drawdown — Optimized")
ax2.fill_between(naive_dd.index, naive_dd.values, 0, alpha=0.3,
                 color="#7B1FA2", label="Drawdown — Naive")
ax2.fill_between(bh_dd.index, bh_dd.values, 0, alpha=0.2,
                 color="#E65100", label="Drawdown — B&H")
ax2.set_ylabel("Drawdown (%)")
ax2.set_xlabel("Date")
ax2.legend(fontsize=8, loc="lower left")
ax2.grid(alpha=0.3)

for ax in axes:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter("%Y-%m"))
    ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mdates.MonthLocator(interval=3))
    plt.setp(ax.xaxis.get_majorticklabels(), rotation=30, ha="right")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Trade log & PnL chart¶

In [436]:
if not trade_log_df.empty and "pnl_est" in trade_log_df.columns:
    close_trades = trade_log_df[trade_log_df["action"].isin(["CLOSE", "STOP-LOSS"])].copy()
    close_trades["date"] = pd.to_datetime(close_trades["date"])
    close_trades.sort_values("date", inplace=True)

    fig, axes2 = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 9),
                              gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": [2, 1]})

    ax3 = axes2[0]
    cum_pnl = close_trades.set_index("date")["pnl_est"].cumsum()
    ax3.plot(cum_pnl.index, cum_pnl.values, lw=2.0, color="#2E7D32")
    ax3.fill_between(cum_pnl.index, cum_pnl.values, 0,
                     where=(cum_pnl.values >= 0), alpha=0.15, color="#2E7D32")
    ax3.fill_between(cum_pnl.index, cum_pnl.values, 0,
                     where=(cum_pnl.values <  0), alpha=0.15, color="#C62828")
    ax3.axhline(0, color="grey", lw=0.8, ls="--")
    ax3.set_ylabel("Cumulative PnL ($)")
    ax3.set_title("Cumulative PnL of Closed Trades", fontsize=13, pad=10)
    ax3.grid(alpha=0.3)

    ax4 = axes2[1]
    colors_bar = ["#2E7D32" if v >= 0 else "#C62828" for v in close_trades["pnl_est"]]
    ax4.bar(range(len(close_trades)), close_trades["pnl_est"].values,
            color=colors_bar, width=0.7, alpha=0.75)
    ax4.axhline(0, color="grey", lw=0.8)
    ax4.set_xlabel("Trade index")
    ax4.set_ylabel("PnL / trade ($)")
    ax4.set_title("Individual Trade PnL", fontsize=11)
    ax4.grid(alpha=0.3, axis="y")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("Not enough closed trades for PnL chart.")

Report¶

In [437]:
print(f"""
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  REPORT — Game 1 NIETO: Multivariate Statistical Arbitrage
  Basket: {str(BASKET_STOCKS):<69s}
  Anchor: {ANCHOR_TICKER:<69s}
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════""")

for k, v in strat_metrics.items():
    bench_v = bh_metrics.get(k, 0)
    naive_v = naive_metrics.get(k, 0)
    try:
        val_s = float(v)
        val_b = float(bench_v)
        val_n = float(naive_v)
        if k in ["Max Drawdown (%)", "Annualized Volatility (%)", "Days in Drawdown"]:
            flag = "(+)" if (val_s > val_b if k == "Max Drawdown (%)" else val_s < val_b) else "(-)"
        else:
            flag = "(+)" if val_s > val_b else "(-)"
    except ValueError:
        flag = "—"
    print(f"  {k:<30} {str(v):>10}  (Naive: {str(naive_v):>10})  (B&H: {str(bench_v):>10})  {flag}")

import os
from google.colab import files

uploaded = files.upload()

for filename in uploaded.keys():
    print(f"\nFichier reçu : {filename}")

    output_code = os.system(f'jupyter nbconvert --to html "{filename}"')

    if output_code == 0:
        html_filename = filename.replace('.ipynb', '.html')

        files.download(html_filename)
